query: Nissan crash test results and any safety awards for Japan-market models

request_params_without_api_key.q: Nissan crash test results and any safety awards for Japan-market models

search_parameters.q: Nissan crash test results and any safety awards for Japan-market models

text_blocks.snippet: Nissan models built for the Japanese market consistently achieve top-tier 5-Star safety ratings and "Five Star Awards" in the rigorous testing conducted by the Japan New Car Assessment Program (JNCAP). These evaluations, managed jointly by Japan's Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism (MLIT) and the National Agency for Automotive Safety and Victims' Aid (NASVA), combine collision safety, preventive safety, and automatic accident emergency call systems into a maximum 190-point scoring matrix.
snippet_highlighted_words: top-tier 5-Star safety ratings and "Five Star Awards"

text_blocks.snippet: Several core models in Nissan’s domestic Japanese lineup have achieved the prestigious 5-Star maximum tier, proving that both their mini-cars (Kei cars) and larger family vehicles provide elite protection:

Nissan Serena: The iconic family minivan secured the JNCAP Five Star Award. Powered by its high-strength "Zone Body" structure and Nissan's 360° Safety Assist suite, it demonstrated near-perfect active intervention.

Nissan Note / Note Aura: These popular e-POWER compact hatchbacks secured a 5-Star evaluation with an exceptional 99% score (81.95 / 82 points) in preventive crash safety and an A-rank in overall collision safety.

Nissan Roox and Dayz: Proving safety is not restricted by vehicle size, Nissan’s flagship Kei cars captured 5-Star ratings. They were highly commended for advanced crash-mitigation tech engineered specifically for compact urban footprints.

Nissan Kicks & Leaf: The compact crossover and pure electric hatchback both maintained a flawless 5-Star tracking streak, hitting peak point metrics for battery frame integrity during high-velocity impacts and advanced pedestian detection.
Beyond standard crash barriers, Nissan’s active preventative engineering has captured specialized domestic automotive accolades in Japan:

list.snippet: RJC Technology of the Year: Awarded directly to the standard-equipped ProPILOT / ProPILOT Assist suites. The advanced autonomous emergency braking and semi-autonomous driving features earned maximum points for handling complex pedestrian and cyclist intersection scenarios.

Japan Car of the Year (Best Innovation Award): Handed to Nissan for its ProPILOT 2.0 infrastructure, which seamlessly integrates high-definition map positioning with 360-degree sensor sweeps to dynamically prevent vehicle-to-vehicle lane collisions.

text_blocks.snippet: Tell me what specific vehicle category (e.g., Kei car, electric vehicle, family minivan) or particular Nissan model you are looking to purchase so I can provide its exact crash test breakdown.

serpapi_response.reconstructed_markdown:
Nissan models built for the Japanese market consistently achieve top\-tier 5\-Star safety ratings and "Five Star Awards" in the rigorous testing conducted by the Japan New Car Assessment Program \([JNCAP](https://www.nasva.go.jp/mamoru/en/about/about.html)\). These evaluations, managed jointly by Japan's Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism \(MLIT\) and the National Agency for Automotive Safety and Victims' Aid \(NASVA\), combine collision safety, preventive safety, and automatic accident emergency call systems into a maximum 190\-point scoring matrix. [3] [5] [6]

### Recent 5\-Star JNCAP Recipients

Several core models in Nissan’s domestic Japanese lineup have achieved the prestigious 5\-Star maximum tier, proving that both their mini\-cars \(Kei cars\) and larger family vehicles provide elite protection: [8]

### Nissan Serena: The iconic family minivan secured the JNCAP Five Star Award. Powered by its high\-strength "Zone Body" structure and Nissan's 360° Safety Assist suite, it demonstrated near\-perfect active intervention.

Nissan Note / Note Aura: These popular e\-POWER compact hatchbacks secured a 5\-Star evaluation with an exceptional 99% score \(81.95 / 82 points\) in preventive crash safety and an A\-rank in overall collision safety. [4]

Nissan Roox and Dayz: Proving safety is not restricted by vehicle size, Nissan’s flagship Kei cars captured 5\-Star ratings. They were highly commended for advanced crash\-mitigation tech engineered specifically for compact urban footprints. [6]

Nissan Kicks & Leaf: The compact crossover and pure electric hatchback both maintained a flawless 5\-Star tracking streak, hitting peak point metrics for battery frame integrity during high\-velocity impacts and advanced pedestian detection. [6] [1] [2]

### Primary Technology & Innovation Awards

Beyond standard crash barriers, Nissan’s active preventative engineering has captured specialized domestic automotive accolades in Japan:

- RJC Technology of the Year: Awarded directly to the standard\-equipped ProPILOT / ProPILOT Assist suites. The advanced autonomous emergency braking and semi\-autonomous driving features earned maximum points for handling complex pedestrian and cyclist intersection scenarios. [5] [0] [9]
- Japan Car of the Year \(Best Innovation Award\): Handed to Nissan for its ProPILOT 2.0 infrastructure, which seamlessly integrates high\-definition map positioning with 360\-degree sensor sweeps to dynamically prevent vehicle\-to\-vehicle lane collisions.

Tell me what specific vehicle category \(e.g., Kei car, electric vehicle, family minivan\) or particular Nissan model you are looking to purchase so I can provide its exact crash test breakdown.
